seo.title: Building Voice Agents with Flowise + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build voice agents with Flowise + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Flowise enables rapid development of voice agents through its drag-and-drop interface for LLM orchestration, combining speech-to-text, reasoning chains, and text-to-speech in real-time pipelines. Guardrails are essential for voice agents due to sub-second latency demands and high-stakes conversational risks like hallucinations or unsafe responses. Vex provides runtime verification to ensure response appropriateness, coherence, and safety without compromising performance.

architectureOverview.heading: Voice Agents Architecture with Flowise
architectureOverview.description:
Voice agents in Flowise typically process real-time audio via speech-to-text (STT) services like Deepgram or Whisper, feeding transcripts into chatflow chains for LLM reasoning. These chains often include vector stores for RAG, custom tools for actions, and multi-agent handoffs, with outputs routed to text-to-speech (TTS) engines like ElevenLabs for immediate playback. The pipeline demands sub-second end-to-end latency, making intermediate validation critical to prevent error propagation.

Flowise chatflows are configured as JSON graphs where nodes represent STT, LLM prompts, tools, and TTS. Vex integrates as a callback handler to monitor node outputs, latency, and context flow without adding pipeline overhead. Common setups use WebSocket endpoints for bidirectional voice streaming, requiring precise error boundaries.

Key challenge: maintaining conversation state across STT chunks, agent handoffs, and TTS synthesis while enforcing safety and relevance at each step.

risks.name: Unvalidated STT chunks producing toxic intermediate prompts
risks.description: Speech-to-text may generate partial transcripts with harmful content before full context is available
risks.mitigation: Vex safety filtering on every STT node output before LLM ingestion

risks.name: Vector retrieval latency exceeding 200ms budget
risks.description: RAG nodes pulling low-relevance docs block real-time voice response
risks.mitigation: Vex latency guards with relevance scoring on retrieved chunks

risks.name: Context loss in multi-agent handoffs
risks.description: Sequential agents drop conversation history during tool calls or reasoning steps
risks.mitigation: Vex conversation coherence checks across agent boundaries

risks.name: Silent tool failures breaking voice loop
risks.description: Custom nodes (API calls, database queries) fail without propagating errors to TTS
risks.mitigation: Vex error propagation and fallback response generation
